description:
## Goal

Prove the complete Markdown Typography Settings promise through the real built desktop application across two process launches, with isolated Workspace and App Settings storage and computed-style assertions on the shared renderer.

Layer tests remain primary for edge cases. This slice exists because Rust, generated TauRPC, Effect state, React navigation, Tauri Store, CSS inheritance, and process restart can all pass separately while the assembled desktop path is broken.

## Dependency and Existing Harness

Requires integrated `bsm-al6.3`.

Reuse the repository WebDriver architecture documented in `docs/agents/webdriver-e2e.md`:

- real debug Tauri binary;
- `@wdio/tauri-service` embedded driver;
- disposable Beadwork repositories created through `bw`;
- typed transport where native OS dialogs are not a stable WebDriver surface;
- scenario runner-owned temporary paths and cleanup;
- no direct hand-editing of Tauri Store files.

Do not treat `browser.refresh()` as an application restart. A refresh reloads the renderer while retaining the same native process. Persistence acceptance requires the first desktop process to exit and a second built-binary process to launch against the same isolated Settings store.

## Isolation Contract

For every full scenario, create one temporary root containing separate paths for:

- Workspace Catalog store, supplied through `BEADSMITH_WORKSPACE_STORE_PATH`;
- App Settings store, supplied through `BEADSMITH_SETTINGS_STORE_PATH`;
- one disposable initialized Beadwork Workspace with representative Issue description and comment Markdown.

The test may pass store paths before process launch but must not write, parse, patch, or seed either store file directly. Workspace state is established through the normal typed Workspace operation. App Settings state is established only through the visible Settings control and normal update pipeline.

Clean up every temporary Workspace/store directory on pass or failure. Scenario order or prior developer preferences must not influence the result.

## Representative Fixture

Reuse or extend the existing populated Issue fixture so one selected Issue contains stable selectors/text for:

- heading and paragraph;
- list;
- inline code;
- fenced code block;
- comment Markdown.

The desktop scenario does not need to duplicate every Markdown syntax test, but it must prove both description and comment call sites receive the same app-wide value.

## Two-Launch Scenario

### Phase 1: set and persist through the real UI

1. Launch the built app with empty isolated Workspace and Settings stores.
2. Before selecting a Workspace, open Settings from the sidebar. This proves Settings is app-level and available in the empty chooser state.
3. Assert the input and preview use the default 14px with no first-run warning.
4. Enter a non-default unambiguous value such as 24 through the number input.
5. Assert the field value is 24 and the live preview Markdown article reports computed `font-size: 24px`.
6. Wait until UI status confirms the newest value is durably Saved. Do not terminate while status is Saving.
7. Establish the disposable Workspace through the normal typed Workspace transport, then return to an Issue List View and select the representative Issue.
8. Assert Issue description and comment Markdown articles report computed `font-size: 24px`; assert one non-Markdown element such as the Settings input or Issue metadata did not inherit 24px.
9. Optionally return to Settings to prove the input remains 24 during same-process navigation.
10. End the first WebDriver run so the Tauri process exits cleanly while preserving both isolated store files.

### Phase 2: restore in a fresh native process

1. Launch a second real built app process with the same isolated Workspace and Settings store paths.
2. Verify the normal Workspace restoration path shows the fixture from the shared isolated Workspace Catalog. Do not re-seed the second process through a typed switch and do not seed files. The required catalog persistence/restoration behavior already exists from closed `bsm-kia.3`; no open Workspace-management bead is a prerequisite.
3. Open Settings and assert the input restores 24 and reports a settled saved/loaded state without re-entering the value.
4. Assert the preview article computed size is 24px.
5. Return to the representative Issue and assert description and comment computed sizes are 24px.
6. Reset to 14px, wait for Saved, and assert preview plus Issue Markdown return to 14px. This proves the Reset path in the assembled binary without requiring a third launch.

The runner can implement phases as two WDIO invocations or two explicit scenario commands sharing the same temporary root. Keep process lifecycle obvious in code and logs.

## Assertions and Selectors

- Prefer semantic labels/roles and existing stable Issue fixture text for navigation.
- For typography, inspect browser-computed CSS `font-size` on the outer Markdown article/module seam. Do not assert only on inline style text or input value.
- Verify one descendant ratio where useful, for example heading/code is larger/smaller relative to a 24px body. Exhaustive ratio arithmetic remains in renderer tests.
- Distinguish preview, description, and comment through accessible regions or stable non-test-only attributes. Adding an accessibility label to the Markdown region is preferable to a test-only global hook when it improves real semantics.
- Do not add screenshot pixel comparison or broad visual snapshots.

## Invalid Input at Desktop Seam

The full invalid matrix belongs in frontend tests. Add one cheap desktop check only if reliable with the native number-input behavior:

- clear the field or enter 7;
- assert validation appears;
- assert preview computed size remains at the last valid value;
- restore a valid value before continuing.

Do not make the process-restart proof flaky merely to duplicate every partial-input case.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The scenario drives the real built Tauri desktop app through WebDriver, not a renderer-only page.
- [ ] Workspace Catalog and App Settings use distinct fresh isolated store paths; no developer or prior-scenario data can leak in.
- [ ] No test directly writes or seeds either store file.
- [ ] Settings opens before any Current Workspace exists and shows default 14px without a first-run error.
- [ ] Direct entry of 24 updates the live preview computed size immediately.
- [ ] The test waits for actual Saved confirmation before ending the first process.
- [ ] Issue description and comment computed sizes become 24px while representative non-Markdown chrome does not.
- [ ] A genuine second native process launched against the same stores restores input, preview, description, and comment at 24px.
- [ ] Reset returns the assembled app to 14px and is durably confirmed.
- [ ] Cleanup removes disposable Workspaces and stores on pass/failure.
- [ ] The focused package command and fixture/process behavior are documented beside existing WebDriver guidance.
- [ ] The focused desktop command passes on the supported CI/development platforms used by the repository.

## Likely Harness Changes

Adapt to the final Workspace-management suite, likely touching:

- a new focused spec under `e2e/settings/` or a clearly separated Settings scenario in the existing issue-list suite;
- scenario runner support for a temporary Settings store and two WDIO process phases;
- a dedicated `wdio.settings.conf.ts` only if that is clearer than parameterizing the existing config;
- `package.json` scripts such as `e2e:settings` and `test:e2e:settings`;
- `docs/agents/webdriver-e2e.md` with exact build/run commands, two-launch behavior, prerequisites, and isolation details;
- the existing representative Beadwork fixture rather than a duplicate fixture factory when reuse stays clear.

Do not make a single giant all-feature WebDriver spec if a focused settings config/spec keeps ownership and failure logs clearer. Reuse shared fixture and embedded-port helpers instead of copying them.

## Verification

- build the debug desktop binary through the established E2E build command;
- run the focused Settings desktop scenario repeatedly;
- run it after an existing non-default developer preference is present to prove isolation;
- run the two phases in their intended order and verify logs identify two distinct launches;
- run existing Issue Explorer/Workspace desktop suites to catch harness regressions;
- run TypeScript/Ultracite checks for harness code.

## Out of Scope

- exhaustive validation/error matrices already covered in module/frontend tests;
- raw store-file migration tests;
- visual snapshots at every supported integer;
- testing OS-native directory picker dialogs;
- font-family preferences;
- performance benchmarking.

## Deviation Policy

Do not weaken the acceptance promise from process restart to renderer refresh, directly seed the Settings store, share the developer store, or assert only the number-input value. If the embedded driver cannot support two launches in one WDIO invocation, change the runner to execute two invocations with shared temporary paths rather than reducing coverage. Document any platform-specific harness limitation and keep the behavioral proof intact.

comments.text:
## Acceptance Review Rationale — 2026-07-15

The original wording said restart, but the current harness commonly uses `browser.refresh()` after direct typed state setup. That only reloads React inside the same native process and cannot prove durable App Settings restoration. This child now requires two explicit built-app launches sharing the same temporary Settings path.

The first launch must set the value through the visible control and wait for actual Saved status before exit. The second launch must observe the value without typing it again. This catches missing `store.save`, wrong app-data resolution, stale generated bindings, startup-load ordering, and renderer propagation bugs that layer tests cannot.

Workspace Catalog and App Settings paths are separate because the feature promises app-wide independence. The scenario may configure paths but must not hand-edit files; otherwise it would bypass the exact serialization/repair interface under test.

Computed CSS on preview, description, and comment is the meaningful assertion. Input value alone proves form state, not that the shared renderer received the setting. A representative non-Markdown element is checked to prevent accidental global body scaling.

The invalid-input matrix remains in frontend tests. One desktop invalid case is optional because native number-input keystroke normalization varies and should not destabilize the higher-value two-launch proof.

If one WDIO service invocation cannot restart cleanly, run two WDIO invocations from the scenario runner with shared temporary paths. The harness must adapt; the acceptance promise must not be reduced.

## Second-Launch Strengthening — 2026-07-15

The second process must restore the disposable Current Workspace from the shared isolated Workspace Catalog rather than invoking `switch_workspace` again. Re-seeding would still prove App Settings persistence, but it would weaken the composed restart story and hide startup-order interactions between Workspace restoration, Settings loading, and first Markdown render. The parent epic waits for `bsm-kia.5`, so normal restoration is a valid prerequisite rather than optional setup.

## Dependency Reassessment — 2026-07-15

The two-launch Settings scenario needs a persisted/restored Current Workspace only so it can inspect Issue Markdown after restart. That capability already landed in closed `bsm-kia.3`. Open `bsm-kia.5` may improve the shared harness and documentation, but reuse is convenience rather than a prerequisite; this task can extend the harness that exists when implementation starts.

title: Prove Markdown settings through real desktop acceptance
